generalities.text: Main THE ENCYCLOPEDIA OF PURE MATERIA MEDICA By TIMOTHY F. ALLEN, A.M., M.D. Presented by Médi-T ANTHRAKOKALI. Prepared by the action of Caustic Potassa upon Fünfkirchen coal. (See Hom. Dispensatory.) Authorities. A. H. Z., 18, 235; (extracted from Polya, Klinger, Isensee, etc.) Several persons proved it (Nos. 12 and 13 , females). MOUTH. ► Tongue soon furred, with nauseous taste, [10] . ► Furred tongue, appetite lost, nausea, [12] . ► Tongue furred, with vomiting and colic, [13] . ► Dryness of mouth and throat, [4] , [8] . ► Taste unpleasant, [5] . ► Taste nauseous, [1] . THROAT. ► Pain in throat, with coated tongue, [9] . ► Burning in throat, with slight dysphagia, [3] . ► Burning in throat, soon followed by anorexia, nauseous taste, and violent colic, without diarrha (after ten days repeated doses), [7] . [10.] ► Dysphagia and dryness of the throat, [10] . STOMACH. ► Anorexia, nausea , vomiting, colic, and flatulence, [2] . ► Burning thirst, [4] . ► Almost unquenchable thirst, [10] . ► Nausea after eating, [3] . ► Nausea, with intercurrent vomiting, [5] . ► Vomiting bilious, mixed with blackish mucus, [13] . ► Sensation of warmth in stomach, [2] . ► Cramps in the stomach, and colic pains, [3] . ABDOMEN. ► Violent burning, deep in the abdomen, [12] . [20.] ► Violent colic, increased at night, so that he had to apply a cataplasm, which relieved (seventh day), [8] . ► Colic, borborygmi, tympanitic distension of the abdomen, [3] . ► Colic, borborygmi, [1] . STOOL AND ANUS. ► Diarrha three times a day, [9] . ► Diarrha instead of usual constipation, [8] . ► Copious diarrha, with colic and borborygmi, [11] . ► Diarrha for several days, with increased urine, [4] . ► Two fluid stools, [13] . ► Stool pasty, preceded by colic and borborygmi, [1] . URINARY ORGANS. ► Burning in urethra on urinating, [2] . [30.] ► Ischuria; urine pale, [12] . ► Urine increased, [5] , [8] , [9] . ► Urine profuse, [1] . ► Profuse watery urine, [10] . ► Copious evacuation of pale, clear urine , [1] , [2] . ► Urine less profuse, [13] . ► Urine pale, acid, profuse, [2] . ► Peculiar odor to the urine, [3] . SEXUAL ORGANS. ► Frequent erections , [1] , [2] , [5] . HEART AND PULSE. ► Pulse and temperature feverish, [12] . GENERALITIES. [40.] ► Feels generally sick, [11] . ► Weakness of whole body and restlessness, with heaviness of the head, [11] . FEVER. ► Creeping chills alternating with heat followed by slight sweat, with accelerated pulse, [3] . ► Increased warmth of body at evening, with accelerated full pulse, [3] . ► Warmth and heat sensation, beginning in the throat, and extending down to the stomach; from hence it migrated to the skin where it did not remain long; was not followed by sweat, [1] . ► Moist skin, [2] . ► Copious sweat in the evening, lasting till morning, with restlessness, headache, prostration, and accelerated pulse, [4] . ► Copious sweat, with slight vascular excitement (sixteenth day), [7] . ► Moderate night-sweat, beginning at evening, with sleeplessness and excited pulse, [11] . ► The burning of the skin was (on eighth day) followed by nightly sweat (continued several days), [1] . [50.] ► Nightly sweat returning every twenty-four hours, as long as the proving lasts, without itching of the skin, [10] . ► Profuse night-sweat, with headache, and accelerated pulse (thirteenth day after increasing doses), [6] . ► Profuse night-sweats, and renewed diarrha, which again ceased without stopping the dosing (seventh day), [8] . ► Copious night-sweat, with loss of sleep, and excessive restlessness, but relief of the pain in the abdomen, [12] . ► Profuse warm night-sweat, with confusion of the head, [13] . ► No sweat, [5] . CONDITIONS. ► Aggravation. ► ( Evening ), Warmth of body, etc.; copious sweat. ► ( Night ), Colic; profuse sweats, etc. ► ( After eating ), Nausea. ► ( Urinating ), Burning in urethra. ► Amelioration. ► ( Cataplasm ), Colic.
